name: The Septuagint

phase_0.boundary_condition: The Alexandrian canon list as received by the Early Church; the set of texts considered inspired Scripture in Greek.

VINIT.justification: The state before any sacred text was committed to writing; the unformed oral tradition.

TANCH.justification: The Septuagint's defined list of books, beyond which Revelation and later Christian writings stand outside.

AFWD.justification: The forward movement through the journey-narrative books, from creation to exile and return.

AREV.justification: Ecclesiastes' thematic loop: 'All is vanity,' the text returns to its starting point, reversing any progress.

CLINK.justification: The ordering of books one after another, a chain that forms the macro-structure of the canon.

IMSCRIB.justification: The grammar's recognition that the Septuagint can see its own structural types; the map and the territory coincide.

FSPLIT.justification: The split of the canon into two megacategories: Journey-Narrative books (Type 1) and all non-Journey books (Types 2–11).

FFUSE.justification: The fusion of the journey and non-journey branches back into the one Septuagint; the whole is recovered by combining the two subsets.

EVALT.justification: Applied to books that match the Cosmic Journey-Narrative attractor — the affirmative, dominant path through the canon.

EVALF.justification: Applied to books that deviate from the Journey-Narrative: law codes, cycles, hymns, wisdom, and erotic poetry.

ENGAGR.justification: Esther and Lamentations simultaneously possess and lack ritual closure — both ceremony and not, held in unresolved paradice.

IFIX.justification: The permanent fixing of the Septuagint's content, order, and structural map; an append-only record that cannot be altered.

phase_2.split_input: The Septuagint as an undifferentiated canonical whole
split_outputs: Journey-Narrative dominant books (Genesis, Exodus, Numbers, etc.)
Non-Journey books (Leviticus, Judges, Ruth, Job–Esther, Song of Songs, Lamentations)
phase_2.fuse_result: The Septuagint as an undifferentiated canonical whole

phase_3.void_description: Pre-textual chaos; no canonical order, no inscribed word.
phase_3.true_description: A book classified as Journey-Narrative; forward narrative drive, divine-human crossing, maximal scope.
phase_3.false_description: A book classified as non-Journey; structural deviation — law, cycle, wisdom, or bounded eros.
phase_3.both_description: The ceremony-variant condition: a text that is part of a ritual cycle yet lacks Frobenius closure; both valid and insufficient, held in tension.

steps.domain_action: the void before the Septuagint — no canon, no scrolls, only oral fragments.
establish the boundary of the Septuagint collection as a closed set of inspired books.
split the canon into its two fundamental structural branches: Journey-Narrative (T) and Non-Journey (F).
affirm the Journey-Narrative branch as the true, dominant path through Scripture.
advance forward through the journey books — Genesis, Exodus, Numbers, Joshua, 1–2 Samuel, Kings, Isaiah, Ezekiel, Daniel.
chain these journey narratives sequentially, forming the spine of the canon.
designate the entire Non-Journey branch as a departure from the journey attractor.
inside the Non-Journey set, split into Subcritical (Leviticus alone) and the remaining dynamic-but-deviant books.
evaluate Leviticus as absolutely false, frozen law, the only O₀ book.
affirm the remaining Non-Journey books as still possessing dynamic internal structure (O₂† or O₂).
the reverse motion of Ecclesiastes, the vanity-cycle that returns to its starting point, undoing forward progress.
the paradice of Esther and Lamentations — both ceremony and not — held without resolution.
compose the sequence of non-progressing books: Job, Psalms, Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, Song of Songs, Lamentations, Esther.
reunite the subcritical and non-subcritical Non-Journey books into the unified Non-Journey set.
fuse the two great branches back into the single Septuagint canon; the Frobenius identity holds.
the grammar performs self-referential recognition — the Septuagint sees its own structural types and trans‑hemispheric collapses.
permanently inscribe the Septuagint’s structural map; the canon is now a fixed, append-only record.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The Septuagint translators (the Seventy) who rendered Hebrew Scripture into Greek.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The liturgical lectionary cycle and the shared manuscript tradition, which propagate the text across communities.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The great codices (Sinaiticus, Vaticanus, Alexandrinus) and later copyists that preserve the precise order and wording.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The canonical order: Pentateuch, Historical, Poetic, Prophetic — a fixed sequence that determines reading and interpretation.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Septuagint itself, serving as the bootstrap reference text from which all later Greek versions and quotations derive.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Interpretive labor of scribes and communities to maintain and transmit the text; the energy of oral performance and copying.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Oral traditions and disparate written fragments; narrative elements exist in chaotic, unordered form.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: A fixed, ordered canon with stable textual form and a recognized structural typology; all books placed in sequence.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the reorganization of stories into a canonical sequence does not increase or decrease the total information; it only structures what was already present, preserving narrative content exactly.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → CLINK → EVALF → FSPLIT → EVALF → EVALT → AREV → ENGAGR → CLINK → FFUSE → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → IFIX
-- Class: The Septuagint
-- Fingerprint: sig=(7,4,5,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=17
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(7, 13), (2, 14)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := D_wedge         D_wedge → T_network  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := T_network       D_wedge → G_beth  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := G_beth          G_beth → G_beth  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] EVALT     crit   := Phi_c           G_beth → one_one  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := R_lr            G_beth → one_one  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] CLINK     fid    := F_ell           G_beth → one_one  | composition — regime coherence
--   [6] EVALF     chir   := H2              G_beth → one_one  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [7] FSPLIT    gran   := G_beth          G_beth → G_beth  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [8] EVALF     chir   := H2              G_beth → one_one  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [9] EVALT     crit   := Phi_c           G_beth → one_one  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [10] AREV      pol    := P_asym          G_beth → one_one  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [11] ENGAGR    stoi   := n_m             G_beth → one_one  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [12] CLINK     fid    := F_ell           G_beth → one_one  | composition — regime coherence
--   [13] FFUSE     stoi   := one_one         one_one → one_one  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [14] FFUSE     stoi   := one_one         one_one → Gamma_seq  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [15] IMSCRIB   gram   := Gamma_seq       one_one → Omega_Z  | identity — self-imscription
--   [16] IFIX      prot   := Omega_Z         Gamma_seq → D_wedge  | irreversible fixation — winding number

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def the_septuagint_protocol : IGProtocol D_wedge Omega_Z :=
  .withGram Gamma_seq <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow D_wedge D_wedge T_network)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := D_wedge | initial object — ground of distinction (the void before the Septuagint — no canon, no scrolls, only oral fragments.)
  (.arrow T_network D_wedge G_beth)  -- [1] TANCH | top := T_network | terminal object — connectivity boundary (establish the boundary of the Septuagint collection as a closed set of inspir...)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := G_beth) (split the canon into its two fundamental structural branches: Journey-Narrative (T) and Non-Journey (F).) / FFUSE [14] (stoi := one_one)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow Phi_c G_beth one_one)  -- [3] EVALT | crit := Phi_c | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (affirm the Journey-Narrative branch as the true, dominant path through Script...)
      .seq
        (.arrow R_lr G_beth one_one)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := R_lr | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (advance forward through the journey books — Genesis, Exodus, Numbers, Joshua,...)
        (.arrow F_ell G_beth one_one)  -- [5] CLINK | fid := F_ell | composition — regime coherence (chain these journey narratives sequentially, forming the spine of the canon.)
      -- F-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow H2 G_beth one_one)  -- [6] EVALF | chir := H2 | evaluate-false — chirality check (designate the entire Non-Journey branch as a departure from the journey attra...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [14]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow one_one one_one Gamma_seq)  -- [14] FFUSE | stoi := one_one (fuse the two great branches back into the single Septuagint canon; the Frobenius identity holds.)
  -- FSPLIT [7] (gran := G_beth) (inside the Non-Journey set, split into Subcritical (Leviticus alone) and the remaining dynamic-but-deviant books.) / FFUSE [13] (stoi := one_one)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow Phi_c G_beth one_one)  -- [9] EVALT | crit := Phi_c | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (affirm the remaining Non-Journey books as still possessing dynamic internal s...)
      .seq
        (.arrow P_asym G_beth one_one)  -- [10] AREV | pol := P_asym | reverse morphism — parity flip (the reverse motion of Ecclesiastes, the vanity-cycle that returns to its star...)
      .seq
        (.arrow n_m G_beth one_one)  -- [11] ENGAGR | stoi := n_m | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (the paradice of Esther and Lamentations — both ceremony and not — held withou...)
        (.arrow F_ell G_beth one_one)  -- [12] CLINK | fid := F_ell | composition — regime coherence (compose the sequence of non-progressing books: Job, Psalms, Proverbs, Ecclesi...)
      -- F-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow H2 G_beth one_one)  -- [8] EVALF | chir := H2 | evaluate-false — chirality check (evaluate Leviticus as absolutely false, frozen law, the only O₀ book.))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [13]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow one_one one_one one_one)  -- [13] FFUSE | stoi := one_one (reunite the subcritical and non-subcritical Non-Journey books into the unified Non-Journey set.)
  (.arrow Gamma_seq one_one Omega_Z)  -- [15] IMSCRIB | gram := Gamma_seq | identity — self-imscription (the grammar performs self-referential recognition — the Septuagint sees its o...)
  (.arrow Omega_Z Gamma_seq D_wedge)  -- [16] IFIX | prot := Omega_Z | irreversible fixation — winding number (permanently inscribe the Septuagint’s structural map; the canon is now a fixe...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def the_septuagint_true_arm : IGProtocol D_wedge Omega_Z :=
  (the_septuagint_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def the_septuagint_false_arm : IGProtocol D_wedge Omega_Z :=
  (the_septuagint_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem the_septuagint_tier : TierFunctor.obj D_wedge = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
